# pm-onethird product roadmap (1/3-2/3 + Union-Closed compatibility-geometry program)

**Headline (2026-07-13).** A NEW arc opened and moved fast: Daniel's spectral / near-ordinal-sum program for 1/3-2/3 (`spectral_near_ordinal_sum_program.tex`, 2026-07-12). PM review + two Daniel-authorized falsification probes (mg-b0a6, mg-3ce3) have **reduced the whole program to a single open lemma, L1b** (transfer the already-proven BK bad-mixing cut into the transport / lambda_std quotient). Everything else is either PROVEN (L1a = Theorem E) or EMPIRICALLY GREEN-BUT-UNPROVEN (T1 distinguished-order, T2 standard-dominance, L4 near-ordinal-sum stability). **This is a viability result, not a proof** — no lemma except L1a was proven; the probes cleared the program to invest proof effort. Standing state otherwise unchanged: AP-2 algebraic-program arc at FULL CLOSE (11 analytic theorems, 4 walls); Frankl arc binding on canonicity; OneThird Cheeger arc strategy-indicted. Awaiting Daniel on L1b direction + the 5 standing decisions. No URGENT-tier signals.

## NEW ARC — Spectral / Near-Ordinal-Sum program (opened 2026-07-12)

**Source.** Daniel emailed himself `spectral_near_ordinal_sum_program.tex` (2026-07-12); saved to iCloud Drive. Full PM read + live state: pm-onethird memory `project_spectral_near_ordinal_sum_program.md`. The program: minimal counterexample P, distinguished order e; symmetrize the linear-extension Cayley walk; pass to its n-dim element-position TRANSPORT standard block S_P (lambda_std = top eigenvalue); argue bad mixing -> lambda_std ~ 1 -> Cheeger low-conductance PREFIX -> near ordinal sum -> balanced pair by minimality -> contradiction. This is the expander/curvature-on-L(P) lens (a sibling to the Cech/F-series cohomological attack, not a replacement).

**Probes run (both Daniel-authorized gate exceptions, both merged to one_third_width_three):**
- **mg-b0a6** (falsification kill-shot, commit d700839): programme ALIVE, no kill-shot. Exhaustive n<=6, 126 posets + N-poset + tight extremals, two independent exact-LE engines (0 mismatches).
- **mg-3ce3** (L4 stress at small leakage / larger n, commit e7e6fb0): L4 GREEN in endgame form. Reached n=16, lambda_std to 0.996 via an order-ideal DP transport matrix (NOT bounded by the n! wall); 6929 posets.

**Status of the chain (discipline: PROVEN vs EMPIRICALLY-GREEN vs OPEN — see memory `feedback_empirical_green_is_not_proven`):**

| Step | What | Status |
|---|---|---|
| identities | transport Laplacian, cut=leakage, exact concatenation, lambda_std>1/3 floor | PROVEN |
| L1a | minimal counterexample -> low-conductance BK cut | PROVEN (Theorem E, thm:cex-implies-low-expansion, step8.tex both repos) |
| T1 | distinguished-order tournament acyclic (>2/3) | EMPIRICALLY GREEN (0 strong cycles / 1063 posets) — unproven |
| T2 | standard dominance (2nd Cayley eigenvalue in standard block) | EMPIRICALLY GREEN (all 126, excess ~1e-15) — unproven |
| L4 | near-ordinal-sum stability (>=1 side keeps a balanced pair) | EMPIRICALLY GREEN endgame form (universal, 0 RED/6929, F(0.10)<=0.35) — unproven |
| L2 | EXACT eigenvector monotonicity | SHOWN FALSE by compute; only the soft version survives (Kendall-tau 0.857) — needs restating |
| **L1b** | **transfer the BK cut into the transport / lambda_std quotient** | **OPEN — the sole remaining gap** |

**L1b is the crux and it is shared.** It is exactly the repo's own cuts-by-pairs open problem (a Buser-type reverse Cheeger for width-3 pair cuts; `one_third_width_three/docs/compatibility-geometry-cuts-by-pairs-scoping.md` §4.3(i)), and it is the same BK-bad-mixing bridge the Cech/F-series program needs. Both programs converge on L1b. b0a6's T2-GREEN is encouraging for its direction but is not the transfer.

**.tex edit noted for Daniel (from mg-3ce3):** state L4 as ">= one side keeps a balanced pair" (endgame form, universal) and let the proof pick the side — the note's Sec 13 emphasis on the SMALLER side alone loses the pair in 89 cases (every one covered by the bigger side).

**Next (IN FLIGHT — gate lifted 2026-07-13):** mg-8b64 = the L1b spot-check, filed + dispatched. Measures whether Theorem E's low-conductance BK pair-cut forces lambda_std~1 in the transport quotient (RED = a poset with low Phi_BK but lambda_std bounded from 1). If GREEN, next ticket = the L1b proof push (Buser-type reverse Cheeger for width-3 pair cuts, theory-grade, shared with the Cech program); then T1/T2/L4 empirical-GREENs need real proofs + L2 restating.

## OneThird Algebraic Program (AP-2) — ARC FULL CLOSE (unchanged since 2026-05-30)

FULL CLOSE on the analytic floor question at currently-known floor-attaining n. Corridor (1/3, 6/17] empirically established + unconditionally analytic-closed at ladder n via the T6-T7-SD0-SD1-SD-UNIQ-SD-PARITY chain. **11 analytic theorems** (T1-T7 + SD-cluster), 4 proven structural walls, doc-set publication-ready. Two named missing inputs (both special cases of the open width-3 conjecture) PARKED research-grade. Reference bars: 1/3 / 6/17 / 134/375 / 4/11 / 14/39 / 8/21 / 2/5 / 27/70 / 1/2. Publishable consolidation: `one_third_width_three/docs/OneThird-AP-2-Prong3H-alpha-Consolidation-AdjReduction-AnalyticAttempt.md@cc745da`; arc-close: `...Prong3I-beta-RungUniqueness-SD-FLOOR.md@3cc2cb3`. No in-scope polecat work remains until Daniel picks write-up (a) vs Missing Input B (c).
